observations.recommendation: To prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the misstatements.

To coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan (SB), to formulate clear internal guidelines and policies for granting AICS and other financial assistance, ensuring alignment with the provisions set forth. These guidelines should establish standardized criteria, assessment procedures, and the appropriate aid amounts to promote consistency, equality, and transparency in distribution and incorporate in the guidelines and policies the manner of maintaining records of AICS grants and even those disapproved in the assessment process to enhance transparency, accountability, and proper documentation of all assistance provided.

To coordinate with the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to properly plan the PPAs to be funded under GAD Fund by establishing a comprehensive GAD Database to identify gender-related issues within the LGU. To stop the inclusion of the AICS program under the GAD budget and only include those determined by the management to be gender-responsive services or activities. To cease from charging AICS against the GAD Fund. To exclude AICS from the GAD Plan and Budget during the next budget preparation cycle. To employ and implement corrective measures to ensure the continuity of the MSWDO's financial assistance program.

To ensure compliance with all safety requirements by contractors and project implementers. This includes equipping workers with proper PPE, clearly marking hazardous areas with appropriate signage and barricades, and restricting unauthorized access and to conduct regular inspections to ensure adherence to safety standards.

To immediately provide the necessary formal training of personnel directly involved in the collection and transport of solid wastes, in accordance with the guidelines set under Section 1 (b) of the IRR of RA No. 9003.

To make sure that all waste collection vehicles are designed to match the road size, condition, and load capacity to ensure safe and efficient waste collection and transport. Vehicles should also have secure covers to prevent waste from spilling during transit. To see to it that all waste collection vehicles are clearly marked with their body number, as well as the name and contact number of the Municipal Government, for easy identification and public awareness in line with regulations; ensure that all waste collection vehicles are properly and visibly marked with the required identification details. To prioritize budgeting for the repair of the non-operational garbage compactor, and consider the cost and benefits before proceeding with the repair.

To cease processing and paying claims for reimbursement related to procurement, except in duly justified and allowable circumstances provided by law. To establish a petty cash fund (PCF) for such disbursements, if deemed necessary.

To inform the concerned suppliers and contractors to file their claims for the release of retention money and to prepare the necessary documents for its release in accordance with Section 62 of the Revised IRR and Section 6.1 and Section 6.2 of Annex E of the same IRR of RA No. 9184, for a given period, otherwise revert the same to the GF in accordance to Section 98 of PD No. 1445. To ensure that recorded claims under the Accounts Payable and the Accounts Payable Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable accounts are valid, fully supported with complete documentation, properly aged, and monitored to identify the accounts that should be derecognized in order to mobilize government funds and promote effective resource planning and allocation.

To consider filling out the vacant plantilla positions from the deserving and eligible casual employees or JOs/COS to reduce hiring of job orders who perform functions of a permanent employee and to ensure accountability of employees; To consider promoting those deserving JOs/COS to at least casual appointments for them to enjoy the benefits of a regular government employee. To examine staffing patterns and determine the necessity of hiring JOs/COS to eliminate possible redundant work. To ascertain that JOs and COS do not perform functions that are exclusive to a regular employee to ensure accountability. To review the existing organizational structure and consider restructuring and the creation of additional plantilla positions to better align its human resource capacity with its growing operational demands to ensure the timely and effective delivery of public services including timely preparation and submission of reports needed by the public and other government agencies.

To ensure that the forms required for procurement and recording are properly and completely filled out for proper documentation, and stronger internal control and compliance with established rules and procedures. To conduct a thorough review of all procurement forms to ensure they are not only properly and completely accomplished.

To ensure the completeness of all the PARs and ICSs for semi-expendable government properties issued to end-users and the renewal thereof every three years as required in COA Circular Nos. 2024-006 and 2022-004 to guarantee accountability for government properties.

To intensify the tax collection strategies by sending notices to all delinquent taxpayers as well as the publication of the entire record with delinquency for awareness of real property owners and administrators and massive tax campaign. To request the SB for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for the collection of delinquencies after the submission of a certified list of all RPT delinquencies which remained unpaid with a statement of the reason for non-collection thereof.

To furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectibles as of December 31, 2024. To undertake periodic reconciliation of the balances per list of RPT delinquencies with that of the RPT and SET Receivable balances per books of accounts to come up with a more accurate and proper balances. Moreover, the Offices of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant will conduct quarterly reconciliation of the list of RPT delinquencies with the RPT and SET Receivable balances per books.

To prepare the Report of SEF Utilization and other required reports in accordance with reporting guidelines and the DILG's posting requirements. To remind the designated LSB member responsible for the submission of the reporting reports to exercise due diligence in ensuring timely submission to Dep-Ed Division’s Office. To assign personnel to monitor the posting of the reports on the transparency billboards and the FDP Portal of the DILG to guarantee compliance with disclosure requirements.

To include the unexpended balance of prior years' LDRRMF in the determination of PPAs under its LDRRMFP in order to achieve accurate financial reporting for the fund. To include the unexpended balance of prior years’ LDRRMF in the formulation and updating of the LDRRMP and its corresponding investment plan.

To initially set-up the impairment of the receivables by journalizing the proposed adjusting journal entries in the books of the Municipality. To initiate the request for write-off of the dormant Accounts Receivables and Other Receivables in the accordance with the procedures laid down in COA Circular No. 2023-008 in order to proceed on the derecognition of these accounts and its related impairment accounts.

To furnish the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) necessary supporting documents to derecognize consumed inventories and ICSs for semi-expendable properties. To require the mandatory submission of RIS to all departments regularly on a weekly basis for consolidation to the SSMI. To submit the SSMI together with the RIS to the MAO for appropriate recording of expense accounts relative to issuances of various inventories. To prepare and maintain complete stock cards of all supplies of the Municipality and reconcile it with the reports provided and kept by the MAO. To initiate the investigation process for the missing inventories and prepare a report therein to establish accountability of officials or employees concerned. To prepare immediately the following proposed adjusting journal entries for the fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements. To apply the Perpetual Inventory Method for the recording of all inventory accounts. To maintain complete supplies ledger cards. To conduct a thorough analysis and review of the inventory accounts and coordinate with the GSO-Designate and other concerned departments to reconcile the inventory records.
To prepare the following proposed adjusting journal entries for the fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
To update records and prepare the property transfer reports (PTRs). To derecognize the properties issued to public schools upon transfer.

To maintain updated PPE and RPLCs for each class of PPE complete with proper description/details and to prepare proper PPELCs for PPEs without existing PPELCs. To update its record of acquisition/disposal/transfer of PPEs in the PPELCs and ensure that the total balance of PPELCs tally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledger. To maintain a complete file of property for each PPE category. To provide various department heads with the necessary form to be used in the inventory taking. To fill out the standard form to be provided by the GSO-Designate to determine their existing PPEs and submit the same to the Inventory Committee. To reconstitute or reorganize the Inventory Committee if necessary and to delegate roles and responsibilities to the members of the committee.
To prepare the following proposed adjusting journal entries for the fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

To observe the disclosure requirements by various IPSAS provisions in the preparation of the FS to meet the completeness of information so that users of the FS could evaluate the significance of the accounts. To prepare the necessary disclosure of certain information required by various accounting standards in the NFS.

To adhere to the guidelines set out by Section 37 of MNGAS for LGUs so that the Municipality may enforce the intended terms, conditions, specifications and requirements for the benefit of the government. To prepare POs complete with basic data, detailed specifications, and brands, if applicable. To validate conformance to the terms specified in the PO and that goods delivered are as indicated in the POs to prevent disparaging effects and/or inconvenience to the operations of the Municipality.

To strictly observe the installation of signboards within the project's location in compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 in order to promote better transparency, accountability and encourage citizen’s participation.

To maintain a special account in the GF for the 20% DF and Tobacco Excise Tax Fund and prepare separate subsidiary ledgers for the accounts to ensure that monitoring of the funds shall be done in a manner that shall be beneficial in the decision-making process of the LGU. To guarantee that the cash for the 20% DF and Tobacco Excise Tax Fund shall have separate reporting and monitoring since the funds are available exclusively for the specific purpose for which they have been created. To facilitate the opening of depository accounts for the 20% DF and Tobacco Excise Tax Fund to segregate the funds.
To prepare the Solid Waste Manual of Procedures to serve as a guide in the efficient implementation of solid waste management programs and activities in the Municipality.

To look for possible alignment or augmentation of funds for the procurement of calibrating tools and equipment. To seek help from the DOST – Ilocos Norte Center on the calibration of all dispensing pumps in the meantime that they do not have the capability to do it. To see to it that all business establishments covered by the Ordinance are inspected and their units are duly calibrated.

To facilitate the appraisal of the road lot components to be recorded as part of the Road Network System. To facilitate the completion of the road map to be maintained by the GSO. To reconcile the local road inventory report and road map with the accounting records. To provide the Municipal Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks.

To facilitate the turn-over of completed infrastructure assets that are no longer under the control of the Municipality to the respective barangays and to derecognize these assets, including the related accumulated depreciation, from the books of the Municipality.

To submit the Deed of Donation of the garbage compactor and all future donations to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the timely recording of donations to the Municipality. After receiving the copy of deed of donation of the garbage compactor, to prepare the proposed Adjusting Journal Entries to record the donation of the garbage compactor. To ensure that all government properties are used appropriately to maximize the returns that the government could get. To utilize government properties according to their intended use.
To test for impairment the dormant receivable accounts.

To post in the PhilGEPS website the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed within the timeframe required by law for all procurement of the Municipality conducted thru public bidding to ensure the Municipality’s full compliance of the procurement law.

To facilitate the timely and efficient implementation of Disaster Prevention & Mitigation and Disaster Preparedness related PPAs to meet the objectives of RA No. 10121. To appoint a full-time LDRRMO that will focus on the strengthening of the disaster resiliency of related communities and enhancing disaster preparedness of the LGU.

To ensure the effective, efficient and timely implementation of the proposed projects under the 20% DF through close monitoring and supervision to guarantee the maximization of the LGUs desired socio-economic and environmental outcomes.

To require the GSO to conduct periodic physical inventory count of materials and supplies and the corresponding Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) be prepared and submitted not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively, and maintain supplies stock cards.
To cancel the Property Acknowledgement Receipt issued on the small tangible items and issue ICS to the end-users to monitor the said items.

To set-up and/or record accounts payable with valid and complete supporting documents in adherence to Section 46 of PD No. 1177 and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445. To revert prior years’ balances of accounts payable which have remained in the books of accounts for two years or more in line with the provision of Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445. To conduct continuous and thorough evaluation of the validity of recorded payables, particularly those pertaining to prior years, in order to determine the immediate appropriate action and/or necessary adjustments therewith.

To prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and request for inspection from proper authorities to determine if the unserviceable and obsolete properties are valueless or with salvage value. After any mode of disposal has been made, to return the properly accomplished Inventory, Inspection and Appraisal form and Report of Waste Materials duly acted upon together with a copy of the OR (if disposal is for value received) to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the dropping of the properties from the Financial Statements in accordance with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
To submit immediately the vouchers and documents to support the entries made in the Journals to enable the Audit Team to make a decision in audit.
To improve the controls and management of the agency’s procurement and consumption of fuel for its motor vehicles by adhering strictly to the regulations provided for under COA Circular No. 77-61.
To submit immediately the vouchers, liquidation reports and documents to support the entries made in the General Journal to enable the Audit Team to make a decision in audit.
To submit the vouchers and documents immediately to ascertain the accuracy and completeness of the recorded transactions.

To ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements as enumerated in Section 9.1.1 and 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and submit the same to the Audit team to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances.

To submit the lacking documents for the transactions and for the BAC to require suppliers/contracts to adhere with the documentary requirements of Annex “H” Appendix A of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 for the LGU’s subsequent transactions involving alternative modes of procurement before any payment is made to avoid audit suspensions.

To intensify the collection of municipal taxes, permit and regulatory fees, service fees and municipal charges within the territorial jurisdiction of the Municipality to be able to generate additional funds to finance priority programs.

To secure a deed of donation from the heirs of Nemesio Judalena that will be the basis of transferring the property in the name of the Municipality. To exert more efforts in locating the Deed of Absolute Sale, which will be the basis of transferring these properties in the name of the Municipality. To transfer the site of Dona Josefa Edralin Marcos Elementary School in the name of the school and to delete the lot in the name of Bimmanga Elementary School from the Inventory of Land. To provide appropriation for the titling of lots and create a committee that will handle the titling of the lots.

To update the RPTAR as basis for preparation of certified list of taxpayers for CY 2013 and onwards, copy furnished the Accounting Office for the recording of the Receivable Accounts at the beginning of the year. Instruct the accountant to adjust or reverse the prior year's balances totally with the certified list of taxpayers submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Vouchers to effect the necessary correcting entries presented in the foregoing discussions in order to correct the misclassified accounts and thereafter to be always guided by the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009 and the IPSASs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant corrected some of the misclassified accounts.
observations.reason: Some items under misclassified PPE are still misclassified as of year-end. This audit finding is reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the: a. GSO to conduct periodic physical inventory count of materials and supplies and the corresponding Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) be prepared and submitted not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively, and maintain supplies stock cards; and b. Municipal Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards to be periodically reconciled with the supplies stocks cards to be maintained by the GSO.
observations.managementAction: The Management is in the process of adhering to the auditor’s recommendation. The Municipal Accountant is yet to maintain a complete supplies ledger cards.
observations.reason: The GSO did not conduct physical count of materials and supplies for CY 2021. The Municipal Accountant is yet to maintain a complete supplies ledger cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the: 1. GSO to cancel the Property Acknowledgement Receipt issued on the small tangible items and to issue ICS to the end-users to monitor the said items; and 2. Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary Adjusting Entries to eliminate the small tangible items and their corresponding accumulated depreciation and ensure that future procurement of small tangible items be accounted for in accordance with COA Circular No. 2005-002.
observations.managementAction: The GSO is yet to cancel the PAR on the small tangible assets and issue ICS to the end-users. The Municipal Accountant has effected the necessary journal entries to remove the small tangible items from PPE accounts.
observations.reason: There was partial implementation. The Municipal Accountant effected the necessary entries to remove the small tangible items from PPE accounts but the GSO has not yet cancelled the PARs and issued ICS to end-users.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the GSO-Designate to reconcile the balances in their respective records and investigate discrepancies where necessary adjustments be made, in order that PPE presented in the financial statements are fully established as to their existence, ownership and correct valuation.
observations.managementAction: The Management is currently planning the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPEs. One of the main goals of this cleansing is to reconcile the respective records of the GSO and the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: One of the main goals of this cleansing is to reconcile the respective records of the GSO and the Municipal Accountant. The RCPPE submitted by the GSO are not yet reconciled with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the: a. Municipal Engineer to facilitate the completion of the road map to be maintained by the GSO and to provide the Municipal Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks; and b. Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the GSO to reconcile the local road inventory report and road map with the Accounting records, and to do physical alternative procedures in determining un-reinstated Road Networks in the books prior to CY 2011, like reconciling the accounting records with the roadmap, to comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2015-008 on the full recognition of Road Networks in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Management is in the process of adhering to the auditor’s recommendation.
observations.reason: The road map is not yet completed as of the writing of this report. The concerned officials are yet to conduct a complete inventory of all local roads of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to: a) Set-up and/or record only accounts payable with valid and complete supporting documents in adherence to Section 46 of PD No. 1177 and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; b) Revert prior years’ balances of accounts payable which have remained in the books of accounts for two years or more in line with the provision of Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445; and c) Conduct continuous and thorough evaluation of the validity of recorded payables, particularly those pertaining to prior years, in order to determine the immediate appropriate action and/ or necessary adjustments therewith.
observations.managementAction: The Management is in the process of adhering to the auditor’s recommendation. Some of the accounts payable recorded from previous years were not supported with complete supporting documents.
observations.reason: Some accounts payable recorded from previous years were not supported with complete supporting documents. There was no reversion made by the management for prior years’ balances of accounts payable which have remained in the books of accounts for two years or more and has no valid supporting documents. There was no action taken by the management during CY 2021 to evaluate the validity of recorded payables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Instruct the GSO-Designate, after any mode of disposal has been made, to return the properly accomplished Inventory, Inspection and Appraisal form and Report of Waste Materials duly acted upon together with a copy of the OR (if disposal is for value received) to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the dropping of the properties from the Financial Statements in accordance with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management is in the process of adhering to the auditor’s recommendation. No IIRUP has been prepared by the GSO – Designate as of the writing of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that   Management:

Instruct the GSO-Designate to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and request for inspection from proper authorities to determine if the unserviceable and obsolete properties are valueless or with salvage value; and


Instruct the GSO-Designate, after any mode of disposal has been made, to return the properly accomplished Inventory, Inspection and Appraisal form and Report of Waste Materials duly acted upon together with a copy of the OR (if disposal is for value received) to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the dropping of the properties from the Financial Statements in accordance with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

observations.reason: No IIRUP has been prepared by the GSO – Designate as of the writing of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exhaust all possible remedies to collect the long overdue accounts. observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Vouchers to effect the necessary correcting entries presented in the foregoing discussions in order to correct the misclassified accounts and thereafter to be always guided by the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009 and the IPSASs.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the:
GSO to conduct periodic physical inventory count of materials and supplies and the corresponding Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) be prepared and submitted not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively, and maintain supplies stock cards; and
Municipal Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards to be periodically reconciled with the supplies stocks cards maintained by the GSO.

We recommended that Management require the:
GSO to cancel the Property Acknowledgement Receipt issued on the small tangible items and issue ICS to the end-users to monitor the said items; and
Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary Adjusting Entries to eliminate the small tangible items and their corresponding accumulated depreciation and ensure that future procurement of small tangible items be accounted for in accordance with COA Circular No. 2005-002.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the GSO-Designate to reconcile the balances in their respective records and investigate discrepancies where necessary adjustments be made, in order that PPE presented in the financial statements are fully established as to their existence, ownership and correct valuation.

We recommended that management instruct the:
Municipal Engineer to facilitate the completion of the road map to be maintained by the GSO and to provide the Municipal Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks; and
Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the GSO to reconcile the local road inventory report and road map with the Accounting records, and to do alternative procedures in determining un-reinstated Road Networks in the books prior to CY 2011, like reconciling the accounting records with the roadmap, to comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2015-008 on the full recognition of Road Networks in the books.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Set-up and/or record only accounts payable with valid and complete supporting documents in adherence to Section 46 of PD No. 1177 and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445;
Revert prior years’ balances of accounts payable which have remained in the books of accounts for two years or more in line with the provision of Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445; and
Conduct continuous and thorough evaluation of the validity of recorded payables, particularly those pertaining to prior years, in order to determine the immediate appropriate action and/ or necessary adjustments therewith.

We recommended that Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to require from the duly authorized representatives a copy of their duly notarized Special Power of Attorney, Board/Partnership Resolution and/or Secretary’s Certificate, whichever is applicable, and to transact only to duly authorized representative of bidders.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to coordinate with all heads of implementing offices to properly plan the timing of their procurement activities and maintain transparency and competitiveness at all times.

We recommended that Management:

require the Municipal Engineer to submit the basis of computation of the ABC and written justification for non-compliance with DPWH Department Order 197, series of 2016; and


strictly adhere with the provisions of the aforementioned rules and procedures in the preparation of the ABC for all succeeding infrastructure projects, otherwise it will be subjected to disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer already prepared the ABCs for Infrastructure Projects in accordance with the guidelines provided in the DPWH Department Order No. 197, series of 2016.
observations.reason: A written reply has been submitted by the Municipal Engineer stating that he erroneously used DPWH DO No. 22, series of 2015 in his computation of ABC.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

ensure that only vacation leave credits are monetized under regular monetization and that all provisions related to the monetization of leave credits are complied with;


instruct the HRMO that undertimes be deducted from the earned leave credits of the employees, if the employee has an available leave credits; otherwise, the undertime be deducted from the salary of the concerned employee; and

require the HRMO to closely monitor the leave credit balances of the employees to avoid having a negative running balances in their respective leave cards. Absences without pay should be deducted from the employee’s salary and not to be presented as a negative balance of the leave credits.
observations.managementAction:
The Management no longer allow monetization of sick leave credits.


The HRMO already records the undertimes of the employees for deduction from their available leave credits or salaries. 


The HRMO already monitors closely the leave credit balances of the employees.
observations.reason:
No regular monetization of sick leave was granted by Municipality during CY 2020.

The HRMO started deducting undertimes from the earned leave credits of the employees.

No negative balances in the leave cards of the employees were noted by the Audit Team for CY 2020.

We therefore recommend the following:

1. Establish more effective procedures in the granting of loans and collection of the same to avoid wastage of government funds. Those beneficiaries with past due accounts should not be granted additional loans nor benefits.
2. Intensify collection of the long overdue receivables. Strict collection strategies must be adapted. If collection proves futile, consider filing of request to write-off.
3. Direct the Accountant to reclassify the receivable from Rodolfo Aganon, former Municipal Treasurer, to Due from Officer and Employees account as prescribed under Section 27 of the Volume III of the Manual on NGAS. observations.managementAction: Recorded all properties acquired through donation.
observations.reason: A Journal entry was made for the adjustment.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the management should comply with the requirements set forth in Section 39 of R.A. No. 9003 in the operation of its controlled dumpsite.

In addition, the municipality’s controlled dumpsite should be converted to a sanitary landfill that shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines set under Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. No. 9003.

observations.reason: Existing dumpsite still in use.
